channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Hunger in Latin America Keeps Falling – Now Healthy Diets Must Become Affordable
item.description: Máximo Torero is the Chief Economist of the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations

item.title: Food and Transport Costs through Strait of Hormuz Remain High, Burdening Vulnerable Economies
item.description: Prior to the latest round of hostilities between the United States and Iran, freight traffic had been increasing in the Strait of Hormuz, and negotiations were underway for a new agreement to fully restore trade through this channel. According to UN Trade and Development (UNCTAD), energy markets were likely to bounce back to normal pre-conflict […]

item.title: Stranded seafarers remain trapped as Hormuz shipping stalls
item.description: As the Iran-US war continues, the key question remains unanswered about how shipping through the Strait of Hormuz will be managed in future – although the UN has reiterated that charging tolls for safe passage runs contrary to international law.

item.title: World News in Brief: New ECOSOC chief, Secretary-General candidates’ debate, torture in Mali, drowning prevention
item.description: Amar Bendjama, Permanent Representative of Algeria to the UN, was elected on Thursday to serve as the next president of the UN’s Economic and Social Council (ECOSOC).

item.title: Gaza’s food gains could unravel without sustained aid, UN warns
item.description: Food security and child nutrition have improved across the Gaza Strip largely due to a scale-up in humanitarian assistance and commercial traffic, but progress remains fragile and could quickly reverse without sustained access, funding and recovery support.

item.title: Satellites can now spot methane leaks in real time – but most go unfixed
item.description: Satellites can now spot major methane leaks from space within days. Yet only 13 per cent of those alerts lead to any response from the governments or companies responsible.

item.title: Security Council LIVE: How to turn preventive diplomacy rhetoric into action
item.description: The Security Council is debating on Thursday how best to settle disputes between States peacefully. Ambassadors will be exploring such themes as effective early warning and how to move from rhetorical support for preventive diplomacy to ensuring more consistent use of its Chapter VI powers under the UN Charter. Follow our live coverage.

item.title: Iran urged to stop protest-related executions
item.description: Independent UN human rights investigators on Thursday called for Iran to immediately halt the execution of 10 men who took part in a protest in the central city of Isfahan earlier this year, warning that some of their relatives have already been called for final visits.

item.title: Syria ‘not alone’ in locating missing persons, tackling displacement
item.description: Syria is not alone in tackling challenges, including the hundreds of thousands who remain missing during past tumultuous decades, the head of a UN institution said on Thursday as António Guterres departs on his first visit to the country in his current capacity as Secretary-General.

item.title: Human-Caused Climate Change, Not El Niño, Drives Most Coral Bleaching Events – Research
item.description: Every morning, as the Indian Ocean retreats from the reef off Zanzibar’s Jambiani village, Chiku Chande steps into the knee-deep lagoon carrying bundles of seaweed seedlings. Years of farming these waters have taught her about every channel and coral outcrop. But lately, the corals that once glowed in brown and gold have become chalky white. […]

item.title: Trump’s Authoritarian Gambit to Muzzle the Foreign Press
item.description: For nearly a century, foreign correspondents have come to the United States precisely because this country prided itself on welcoming scrutiny. Now, the Trump administration proposes to turn that legacy on its head—by transforming visas for international journalists into instruments of control

item.title: Don’t Cry for Milei, Argentina
item.description: Sunday’s World Cup final focused world attention on the stench of corruption, past and current, surrounding some of its main protagonists, including Argentina, and the presidents of FIFA, Argentina and the main host nation. Messi football Nick Corbishley has exposed the corruption of World Cup football and Argentina’s Milei government, enthusiastically supported by US President […]
